title: Lactuca koshunensis
intro: Lactuca koshunensis is a species of lettuce native to the mountainous regions of Taiwan, characterized by its unique, edible leaves and stems. The leaves of Lactuca koshunensis possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Lactuca koshunensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Lactuca koshunensis is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Lactuca koshunensis is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is classified under the family Asteraceae and is native to the mountainous regions of Taiwan. The genus Lactuca is a group of plants commonly known as wild lettuces. The species Lactuca koshunensis is often found in high-altitude habitats. This plant is a member of the tribe Cichorieae within the family Asteraceae.
